steps.title: Trace the passage's two-part structure
steps.content: The passage opens with “Hiroshi Senju is known worldwide” — naming a specific artist — then spends the rest explaining what makes his waterfall paintings distinctive: their large scale, his choice to show only part of the waterfall, and his use of mineral pigments. That is two clear moves: introduce the artist, then describe characteristics of his paintings.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — It introduces an artist and then explains some common characteristics of well-known paintings by that artist.: Matches the two-part structure exactly — artist introduction followed by details about his paintings. ✓
  • Choice B — It explains a specific painting technique and then provides examples of artists who use the technique.: The order is reversed — the passage names Senju first, not a technique, and discusses only one artist. ✗
  • Choice C — It describes a famous painting and then compares it to a lesser-known painting from the same time period.: No comparison between two paintings appears; the passage discusses Senju’s style generally. ✗
  • Choice D — It gives an opinion on an artist and then suggests multiple reasons why the artist's work has been largely overlooked.: The passage explicitly says Senju is “known worldwide” — the opposite of overlooked. ✗
A is correct.
